siteFooter.tagline: A seeded voxel world protocol on Solana.

hero.title: Program a forged chip, then watch its pins drive devices.
hero.body: Write Arduino sketch code. The browser compiles it with AVR GCC to Intel HEX, then avr8js executes it on an ATmega328P simulation.

viewport.title: Every forged pin can become a game-world device port.

components.ready: Drag a device into the board. Select Wire to route insulated wires.
components.dragLed: Drag the LED tool into the board, then drag placed LEDs to reposition them.
components.dragButton: Drag the button into the board. Hold the cap to conduct, release to open the circuit.
components.dragSwitch: Drag the switch into the board. Click the lever to latch ON or OFF.
components.dragMotor: Drag the motor into the board. It spins when one terminal is powered and the other reaches GND.
components.dragBuzzer: Drag the buzzer into the board. It vibrates when powered through a GND return path.
components.pickFirst: Wire mode: click a chip pin or component pin.
components.pickSecond: Wire mode: click the board to add bends, click the second endpoint to connect, or right-click to end.
components.waypointAdded: Wire bend added. Click another board point, click an endpoint, or right-click to end.
components.switchOn: Switch latched ON. Its two terminals are now conducting.
components.switchOff: Switch latched OFF. Its two terminals are now open.

concept.title: Game integration path
concept.body: Forge the chip body, store compact code, attach devices to pins, then let the simulator drive lights, doors, traps, relays, or factories.

errors.emptyProgram: Enter Arduino code, Intel HEX, or AVR assembly first.
errors.noHex: The compiler did not return Intel HEX output.
errors.flashOverflow: Compiled firmware uses {bytes} B, above the Uno limit of {limit} B.
errors.hexChecksum: Intel HEX checksum failed on line {line}.
errors.hexEof: Intel HEX is missing the EOF record.
